title: Autonomous methods and systems for tying surgical knots
abstract: A robotic suture knot tying system includes a first robotic arm, a second robotic arm, a suturing device including a suturing end effector operatively coupled to the first robotic arm, and a suture handling device operatively coupled to the second robotic arm. The system also includes a control system operatively coupled to the first robotic arm, the second robotic arm, the suturing device, and the suture handling device and configured to control operation thereof to cooperatively perform a suture knot tying procedure on a patient.
claims:
Claims 1. A robotic suture knot tying system, comprising: a first robotic arm; a second robotic arm; a suturing device including a suturing end effector, said suturing device being operatively coupled to the first robotic arm; a suture handling device operatively coupled to the second robotic arm; and a control system operatively coupled to the first robotic arm, the second robotic arm, the suturing device, and the suture handling device and configured to control operation thereof to cooperatively perform a suture knot tying procedure on a patient. 2. The system of claim 1, wherein the suture handling device comprises: an elongated tubular housing having a proximal end coupled to the second robotic arm and an opposite distal end, the housing including a first channel and a second channel therein, each channel extending longitudinally along the length of the tubular housing; a first suture grasping instrument in the first channel of the housing, the first suture grasping instrument having an end effector that can be actuated to extend outwardly from the distal end of the housing to grasp and manipulate a suture; and a second suture grasping instrument in the second channel of the housing, the second suture grasping instrument having an end effector that can be actuated to extend outwardly from the distal end of the housing to grasp and manipulate the suture. 3. The system of claim 2, wherein the suture handling device further comprises a cutting blade recessed in the distal end of the housing at a location between the first and second channels, wherein the first and second suture grasping instruments can be actuated to retract into the distal end of the housing while grasping separate portions of the suture and to engage the suture with the cutting blade to sever the suture between the separate portions of the suture. 4. The system of claim 2, wherein the elongated tubular housing includes a third channel extending longitudinally along the length of the tubular housing, and wherein the device further comprises a laparoscope in the third channel in the tubular housing. 5. The system of claim 2, wherein each of the first and second suture grasping instruments includes a jaw member pivotally connected to another jaw member, each jaw member having a suture engagement surface facing the suture engagement surface of the other, wherein one of the jaw members includes a suture pivot post extending from its suture engagement surface, and the other jaw member has an opening in its suture engagement surface sized and arranged to receive the suture pivot post, wherein the jaw members can be positioned relative to each other in a suture grasping mode in which the suture pivot post is received in the opening and the suture engagement surfaces securely engage the suture to inhibit movement of the suture relative to the grasper, and wherein the jaw members can be positioned relative to each other in a pulley mode in which the grasper slidably supports the suture and the suture pivot post acts as a pivot point and bearing surface for the suture. 6. The system of claim 1, wherein the robotic suture knot tying system is autonomous. 7. The system of claim 1, wherein the robotic suture knot tying system is surgeon- controlled. 8. A method for tying a surgical knot on tissue of a patient using a robotic suture knot tying system including a suturing device having a suturing end effector and a suture handling device having first and second suture grasping instruments, the method comprising the steps of: throwing a stitch at a target location on the tissue patient using the suturing end effector to form first and second suture lines extending out of the target location; grasping the first suture line with one of the first and second suture grasping instruments and cutting the first suture line; manipulating the suturing end effector and the first and second suture grasping instruments to tie a suture knot from the first and second suture lines; and tightening the suture knot onto the tissue using the first and second suture grasping instruments. 9. The method of claim 8, wherein the suture handling device includes a housing with a cutting blade therein, and wherein cutting the first suture line comprises grasping the first 

 suture line by the first and second suture grasping instruments at spaced apart locations and retracting the first and second suture grasping instruments into the housing to engage the first suture line with the cutting blade to sever the first suture line. 10. The method of claim 8, wherein the method is performed autonomously. 11. The method of claim 8, further comprising tying a series of surgical knots on the tissue of the patient. 12. The method of claim 8, wherein the surgical knot is a square surgical knot. 13. The method of claim 8, wherein each of the first and second suture grasping instruments includes a jaw member pivotally connected to another jaw member, and wherein manipulating one of the suture grasping instruments comprises: positioning the jaws of the surgical grasper on opposite sides of the suture; moving the jaws relative to each other to a grasping mode position in which the grasper fixedly holds a portion of the suture and inhibits movement of the suture relative to the grasper for one part of the knot tying process; and moving the jaws relative to each other to a pulley mode position in which the grasper slidably supports the suture at a pivot point and permits the suture to slide relative to the grasper for another part of the knot tying process. 14. A robotic suture knot tying system, comprising: a first robotic arm; a second robotic arm; a suturing device including a suturing end effector, said suturing device being operatively coupled to the first robotic arm a suture handling device operatively coupled to the second robotic arm, wherein the suture handling device comprises: (a) an elongated tubular housing having a proximal end coupled to the second robotic arm and an opposite distal end, the housing including a first channel, a second 

 channel, and a third channel therein, each channel extending longitudinally along the length of the tubular housing; (b) a first suture grasping instrument in the first channel of the housing, the first suture grasping instrument having an end effector that can be actuated to extend outwardly from the distal end of the housing to grasp and manipulate a suture; (c) a second suture grasping instrument in the second channel of the housing, the second suture grasping instrument having an end effector that can be actuated to extend outwardly from the distal end of the housing to grasp and manipulate the suture; and (d) a laparoscope in the third channel in the tubular housing; and a control system operatively coupled to the first robotic arm, the second robotic arm, the suturing device, and the suture handling device and being configured to control operation thereof to cooperatively perform a suture knot tying procedure on a patient. 15. The system of claim 14, wherein the suture handling device further comprises a cutting blade recessed in the distal end of the housing at a location between the first and second channels, wherein the first and second suture grasping instruments can be actuated to retract into the distal end of the housing while grasping separate portions of the suture and to engage the suture with the cutting blade to sever the suture between the separate portions of the suture. 16. The system of claim 14, wherein each of the first and second suture grasping instruments includes a jaw member pivotally connected to another jaw member, each jaw member having a suture engagement surface facing the suture engagement surface of the other, wherein one of the jaw members includes a suture pivot post extending from its suture engagement surface, and the other jaw member has an opening in its suture engagement surface sized and arranged to receive the suture pivot post, wherein the jaw members can be positioned relative to each other in a suture grasping mode in which the suture pivot post is received in the opening and the suture engagement surfaces securely engage the suture to inhibit movement of the suture relative to the grasper, and wherein the jaw members can be positioned relative to each other in a pulley mode in which the grasper slidably supports the suture and the suture pivot post acts as a pivot point and bearing surface for the suture. 17. The system of claim 14, wherein the robotic suture knot tying system is autonomous. 18. The system of claim 14, wherein the robotic suture knot tying system is surgeon- controlled.
